conbody.p: This section describes issues and limitations present in Juniper Cloud-Native Router (JCNR) release 24.1.

section.title: Known Issues and Limitations in Juniper Cloud-Native Router Release 24.1

li.p: JCNR-6166: Modifying the route-distinguisher of an active routing-instance is not supported— JCNR does not support modifying the route-distinguisher of an active routing-instance and the event is considered catastrophic. To modify the route-distinguisher deactivate the routing-instance, make the change and activate the routing-instance.

ul.li: JCNR-6339: JCNR configured with BGP unnumbered peering on L3 sub-interfaces intermittently causes component pods to crash—When L3 VLAN sub-interfaces are configured on JCNR with BGP unnumbered peering, the cRPD and vRouter pods may crash intermittently when restarted. The pods may recover themselves or may require another restart.

li.p: JCNR-6383: Routes are not propagating between VRF changes for an interface— When an interface is moved to a different VRF, any existing routes are not properly reconfigured since the ARP or NDP nexthops are resolved using the interface's previous VRF.

JCNR-6211: Setting an MTU higher than the recommended 1500 bytes on JCNR interfaces for Microsoft Azure Cloud Platform deployments results in BGP flapping— Azure Cloud Platform recommends an MTU <=1500 on interfaces. If JCNR is deployed using a fabric interface with MTU higher than 1500 bytes, it results in packet drops while sending BGP updates and eventually BGP flapping.

ul.li: JCNR-4248: Keepalived cores are generated when VRRP is initially configured in cRPD— When VRRP is initially configured in cRPD or deleted and re-configured, keepalived cores are generated in the /var/crash directory.
